---
title: "Examples"
---

# Examples

This page collects a small set of paper-aligned figures used on the site. The
goal is not to reproduce every panel, but to keep a few clear visual anchors
for the package.

::: {.example-block}
## Toy line profiles

![Toy profile comparison strip.](site-assets/toy_case_row1.png)

The mock-profile experiments compare visually distinct line shapes in settings
where classical summaries can become degenerate, while low-order path
coordinates remain sensitive to ordered morphology.
:::

::: {.example-block}
## Mixed emission--absorption profiles

![Signed emission-absorption ordering strip.](site-assets/signed_emabs_cases_panel_boundary_regions_row1.png)

For mixed-sign profiles, the emission--absorption ordering coordinate
`p_pm` provides a compact way to distinguish profiles that differ mainly
in the order in which emission and absorption appear along the spectral axis.
:::

::: {.example-block}
## IFU kinematic example

![Single-galaxy IFU kinematic crop.](site-assets/kinematics_col1_full.png)

The IFU example shows how path-derived features can be used as a segmentation
space before estimating representative velocities from stacked spectra.
:::
